webLinks.linkText: White Bay Power Station - Biennale of Sydney

properties.publicTransport: Route 441 and 442 buses run regularly to Rozelle. As well, route 443 heritage buses will run on weekends and public holidays. See details at transportnsw.info
properties.otherAdvice:
The Biennale of Sydney will be held at White Bay Power Station open from Tuesday-Sundays 10am-5pm daily, from Saturday 14 March to Sunday 14 June. The exhibition will also be open on public holidays.

Traffic will be heavy on roads approaching White Bay Power Station as crowds travel to and from the event,

Attendees driving should carpool with their group, plan to arrive early as local parking is limited.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on Victoria Rd, the City West Link and Robert St.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Guy Sebastian at the TikTok Entertainment Centre from Thursday 30 April to Saturday 2 May. Shows start at 7pm nightly.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as attendees travel to and from the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.publicTransport: Event tickets include travel on public transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Caravan Camping Holiday Supershow 2026 at Rosehill Gardens. Start daily at 9:30m, finish Tuesday to Saturday at 5pm and Finish Sunday at 4pm

Traffic may be heavier than usual on James Ruse Dr and Hassall St as attendees arrive and leave the event.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads in the area.

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on trains. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Sharks v Wests Tigers at Ocean Protect Stadium in Woolooware. Jersey Flegg match at 1.40pm, NRL kick-off at 4.05pm, full time around 6pm.

Traffic on roads approaching the stadium will be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Captain Cook Dr, Woolooware Rd and Gannons Rd.

Captain Cook Drive will be closed in the immediate vicinity of the Stadium from 15-minutes before full-time until 30-minutes after full time (45-minute closure). 

All road closures will be implemented and managed by NSW Police. Allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity in the area.

properties.publicTransport: Shuttle bus services will operate for the NRL game. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL Knights v Rabbitohs at McDonald Jones Stadium. NSW Cup match at 11.30am & NRL kick off at 2pm.

Traffic may be heavier than usual as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time on Turton Rd, Griffiths Rd, Newcastle Rd, Lambton Rd and Bridges Rd through Broadmeadow while fans are making their way to and from the stadium.

For passenger drop-off, use the allocated 10-minute loading zone in the McDonald Jones Stadium car park. Entry is via the driveway off Turton Road.

Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets and additional light rail services will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Roosters v Broncos at Allianz Stadium. Kick-off at 7.35pm, full time at 9.25pm.

Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as fans make their way to the precinct

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 4.30pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.   
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas will be maintained from Lang Road. Grass car park open from 4.35pm.   
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to 11pm on sections of Albion Street, Fitzroy Street and Lang Road, and from 6am to 11pm on Cleveland Street.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
Clearways are strict no-parking zones and also apply to local residents and Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs before parking as vehicles left in special event clearways will be towed and a towing fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator. 

From 6am to 11pm 

  • Cleveland Street between South Dowling Street and City Road (southern side) 
  • Cleveland Street between Abercrombie Street and South Dowling Street (northern side) 

From 3pm to 11pm 

  • Albion Street between Bourke Street and Flinders Street (both sides) 
  • Lang Road between Anzac Parade and Errol Flynn Boulevard (northern side)
  • Fitzroy Street between South Dowling Street and Bourke Street (northern side)
arrangementElements.title: Special Event Clearways

roads.mainStreet: Moore Park Road, Anzac Parade, Lang Road, Eastern Distributor

properties.otherAdvice:
Sydney Showgrounds will host multiple festivals from Friday 1 to Saturday 2 May:

  • Meatstock on Friday 1 & Saturday 2 May - Friday starts at 4pm, Saturday starts at 1pm, finishes nightly at 11pm
  • Midnight Mafia on Saturday 2 May - Starts at 5pm, finishes at midnight

It’ll be busy in Olympic Park on Friday with up to 20,000 for a nearby NRL game, and on Saturday with a sold-out crowd of more than 20,000 ravers expected alongside thousands of people attending other events in nearby venues.

Attendees are strongly encouraged to plan their trip and consider all the available travel options ahead of time.  

Motorists should allow plenty of extra travel time on roads approaching Olympic Park including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close local roads in the precinct and traffic congestion is expected before and after the events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.otherAdvice:
Special event clearways will be in place at the following locations to facilitate Sydney Trains trackwork replacement bus operations:

  • Chalmers Street, Surry Hills. western side of Chalmers Street between Cleveland Street and Devonshire Street and eastern side between Cleveland Street and Rutland Street.

Special event clearways are strict no-parking zones and also apply to local residents and Mobility Scheme Parking Permit holders. Vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies.

The Enchanted Forest is returning to Shellharbour from Friday 24 April until Sunday 17 May.

For the duration of the event, a speed reduction will be in place on Shellharbour Rd.

webLinks.linkText: TfNSW Vivid Sydney - top tips to plan your trip

properties.publicTransport: Trains will not drop off at Circular Quay from 6pm-11pm on Saturdays + Sun 7 Jun. Buses will relocate to Martin Place from 6pm Fridays and 5pm on weekends. Light rail will not run between Circular Quay and Town Hall from 6pm Fridays and 5pm on weekends. Ferries will be busy on weekends and access to wharves at Circular Quay will be different on busy nights – follow signage. For more details and top tips to plan your trip, visit transportnsw.info.
properties.otherAdvice:
Vivid Sydney returns to light up the city for its 16th year across 23 consecutive nights from Friday 22 May to Saturday 13 June.

The city is no place for cars with road closures and clearway parking restrictions in place each night in Circular Quay, The Rocks, Dawes Point and Millers Point between 3pm and midnight daily, and until 1am on Saturday nights.

Drone Shows are also returning this year but will take place at Cockle Bay in Darling Harbour twice a night on Sunday to Wednesday nights only (excl Sun 7 June).

Road closures:

  • Monday to Thursdays: from 3pm local roads in The Rocks, Millers Point and Dawes Point will close including sections of Lower Fort St, Merriman St and Rodens Ln. From 5pm additional roads will close including sections of George St, Argyle St and Argyle Pl, Harrington St and Pottinger St. On Mon-Wed drone show nights only, closures will also be in place from 7pm on Wheat Rd at King St Wharf/Cockle Bay,
  • Fridays: additional closures will be in place from 3pm on High St and High Ln. From 5pm additional closures will be in place including all of Argyle St, and sections of Hickson Rd, George St, Essex St, Upper Fort St and Watson Rd. From 6pm roads in Circular Quay including Pitt, Loftus, Young, Albert and Phillip Streets will close.
  • Saturdays + Sun 7 Jun: local closures will be in place from 3pm, and major roads in Circular Quay, The Rocks, Millers Point and Dawes Point will close from 5pm. Additionally Grosvenor St/Bridge St between York St and Loftus St will be closed - no east/west access via Bridge St during this time. Roads remain closed until 1am. Wheat Rd will close from 7pm to 11pm.
  • Sundays + Mon 8 Jun: local closures will be in place from 3pm, and key roads in Circular Quay, The Rocks, Millers Point and Dawes Point will close from 5pm to midnight listed above. On Sunday drone show nights only, closures will also be in place from 7pm on Wheat Rd at King St Wharf/Cockle Bay,
  • 12pm Friday to 1am Monday each weekend, George St will close between Lower Fort St and Hickson Rd for The Rocks Markets. 

Special event clearways will be in effect on most major roads in the city between 3pm and midnight daily during Vivid Sydney. Some clearways will be in effect earlier from 1pm and 2pm on local roads.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

roads.mainStreet: George St, Hickson Rd, Bridge St
roads.suburb: Circular Quay, The Rocks, Barangaroo, Darling Harbour

properties.otherAdvice: Gosford Strip Precinct Block Party will take place on Sunday 3 March from 12pm to 8pm. Temporary bus stop will be in place in Erina St.

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in AFL tickets. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
With up to 40,000 AFL fans expected to attend the Swans v Melbourne, along with multiple The Wiggles shows at neighbouring Hordern Pavilion, motorists are urged to avoid unnecessary travel in the area where possible.

  • AFL: Sydney Swans v Melbourne at the SCG. Gates open at 1.45pm, first bounce from 3.15pm and full time at 5.55pm.
  • The Wiggles at Hordern Pavilion. Shows at 10am, 1pm and 4pm.
  • Le Marché French Markets at the Entertainment Quarter from 9.30am until 2pm.

Significant traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park so motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Major roads will also be closed in the Sydney CBD between 4am and 12.30pm for the annual Runaway Sydney Half Marathon. Fans travelling to the game from the north should expect southbound delays approaching the Sydney Harbour Bridge and Tunnel while the Cahill Expressway is closed. See details here.

Attendees are urged to catch public transport and leave the car at home to avoid significant traffic delays and the high demand for parking.  

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 11.15am and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.  
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP2 Kippax car park will be maintained via Moore Park Road and access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas will be maintained from Lang Road. Grass car parks open from 12.15pm. 
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 11am to 8pm on sections of Albion Street, Fitzroy Street, Lang Road and Moore Park Road, and from 6am to 8pm on sections of Cleveland Street.  

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 11am to 8pm on sections of Albion Street, Fitzroy Street, Lang Road and Moore Park Road, and from 6am to 8pm on sections of Cleveland Street.  

From 6am to 8pm 

  • Cleveland Street between South Dowling Street and City Road (southern side) 
  • Cleveland Street between Abercrombie Street and South Dowling Street (northern side) 

From 11am to 8pm 

  • Albion Street between Bourke Street and Flinders Street (both sides) 
  • Fitzroy Street between South Dowling Street and Bourke Street (northern side) 
  • Lang Road between Anzac Parade and Errol Flynn Boulevard (northern side) 
  • Moore Park Road between Flinders Street and Greens Road (northern side) 

Special event clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.
arrangementElements.title: Special Event Clearways

roads.mainStreet: Moore Park Road, Anzac Parade, Lang Road, Eastern Distributor

properties.otherAdvice:
Deftones at Qudos Bank Arena on Saturday 2 and Sunday 3 May. Show starts nightly from 7pm, finish arounf 10.30pm.

It’ll be busy in Olympic Park on Saturday with a sold-out crowd of more than 20,000 ravers expected alongside thousands of people attending other events in nearby venues.

Traffic delays are expected as attendees make their way to Olympic Park each day. Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

Ticketholders driving should pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space, available until 11:59pm the night before the show unless sold out prior. If you have pre-booked your parking, follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Road closures may be in place. Check out the Sydney Olympic Park Authority interactive map for more details.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Eels v Warriors at CommBank Stadium in Parramatta. NRL kick-off at 5.30pm, full time around 7.15pm.

With thousands of fans expected, traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and nearby car parks are expected to be in high demand.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 7.15pm until 7.45pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Panthers v Sea Eagles at CommBank Stadium in Parramatta. NRL kick-off at 6.15pm, full time around 8pm.

With thousands of fans expected, traffic will be heavy on roads approaching Parramatta and nearby car parks are expected to be in high demand.

Allow extra travel time on roads including Parramatta Road, Church Street, Victoria Road, James Ruse Drive and Pennant Hills Road. 

Motorists should also exercise caution as pedestrian activity will be high on roads between Parramatta Interchange and the stadium. 

Drivers are reminded that light rail services are running through Parramatta CBD. Trams can’t stop quickly or easily – so exercise extra caution when driving in the area. 

Road closures 

Roads around the stadium will close from 8pm until 8.30pm approx. to allow crowds to safely leave the venue. Roads will re-open once crowds have cleared the area. 

  • O’Connell Street from Grose Street to George Street 
  • Ross Street from O’Connell Street to Trott Street 
  • Victoria Road from O’Connell Street to Villiers Street 

While closures are in place motorists are encouraged to avoid driving through Parramatta CBD and detour around the closures via Grose Street, Brickfield Street, Victoria Road and Wilde Avenue in both directions.

properties.publicTransport: Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
May shows at the TikTok Entertainment Centre:

  • Sunday 3 May: Jimmy Carr at 8pm
  • Saturday 9 May: Dr. Satinder Sartaaj at 7.30pm
  • Saturday 16 May: FIVE at 8pm
  • Monday 18 & Tuesday 19 May: Split Enz - Forever Enz Tour 2026. Shows start nightly at 8pm
  • Wednesday 20 & Thursday 21 May: Michelle Obama. Shows starts nightly at 7.30pm
  • Saturday 30 May: Nancy Ajram at 7.30pm

Traffic may be heavier than usual as attendees travel to and from the event. Motorists should allow extra travel time and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

From Friday 22 May, traffic delays are expected as Vivid takes place in and around the Harbour Foreshore. Ticketholders to ICC events are urged to plan ahead if attending a show while Vivid is also on.

properties.publicTransport: Routes 441, 504 and 506 will be diverting while road closures are in place. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Thousands of participants will take part in the 5km and 10km Mother's Day Classic fun run and walk events starting from 7am in The Domain. Participants will travel along sections of Macquarie St, College St then Art Gallery Rd and Mrs Macquarie's Rd to finish back in The Domain by approximately 12pm.

Mother's Day is traditionally one of the busiest days on the roads. Wherever you are travelling, expect it to take longer than usual and factor in extra travel time. 

ROAD CLOSURES

  • 5am to 12pm midday: Art Gallery Rd/Mrs Macquarie's Rd from Prince Albert Rd. 
  • 5am to 10.30am: Hospital Rd and a section of Prince Albert Rd. Access to Sydney Hospital and Hospital Rd is via Shakespeare Pl only in both directions.
  • 6am to 10.30am: Macquarie St between St James Rd and Hunter St, College St between Park St and Prince Albert Rd and sections of St James Rd, Prince Albert Rd, St Mary's Rd and Yurong Pkwy will be closed.

Special event clearways will be in effect from 5am to 10.30am on sections of Macquarie St, Hunter St, College St, Prince Albert Rd, St Mary's Rd and St James Rd. Clearways will also be in effect on sections of Prince Albert Rd and Hospital Rd from 5am to 12pm midday.

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

roads.mainStreet: Macquarie St, Prince Albert Rd, College St, Art Gallery Rd

properties.otherAdvice: The Coffs Harbour Agricultural Show will be held from Friday 1 May to Saturday 2 May at the Coffs Harbour Showground. Friday 4pm-8pm, Saturday 4pm-8pm. For the safety of pedestrians, motorists are advised there will be a southbound shoulder closure and no right turn from the northbound lane of the Pacific Highway.

Major road closures will be in place on Sunday 3 May between 4am and 1pm in the Sydney CBD, The Rocks, Barangaroo and Pyrmont for the Runaway Sydney Half Marathon. Half Marathon runners will be released in waves from 6.25am and the 10km event will start at 9.55am. Both courses start on the Cahill Expressway at Macquarie St near Circular Quay and will end at The Domain Sydney by 12.30pm.   

Motorists are encouraged to avoid non-essential travel in the CBD and Pyrmont areas during the event as delays are expected. If you must drive, diversions will be in place so allow extra trave time and follow detour signs. 

Key closures: 

  • Cahill Expressway Circular Quay Overpass – 4am to 12.30pm: closed in both directions between the Harbour Bridge and Domain Tunnel, including the on and off ramps at Macquarie St. Motorists travelling towards the Eastern Distributor/Eastern Suburbs should use the Sydney Harbour Tunnel while the Cahill Expressway Overpass is closed. 
  • Sydney CBD – 4am to 12.30pm: Macquarie Street will be closed from Albert St to Hyde Park. Motorists can cross Macquarie St at Shakespeare Pl / Bent St before 5.30am and after 12pm only. After 12pm, there is no access to or from Macquarie St; access to the Eastern Distributor will be via William St. Sections of Bridge St, Conservatorium Rd, Hunter St and Phillip Ln. Motorists leaving the city can access the Harbour Bridge via Clarence St or Kent St.  
  • Pyrmont, Barangaroo & King St Wharf – 5am to 10.30am: the Western Distributor will be closed from Pyrmont to the CBD, including the Fig St and Pyrmont St on-ramps and the King St off-ramp. There is NO direct access from the Anzac Bridge to the Sydney Harbour Bridge – citybound traffic on the Anzac Bridge will be detoured via the Bathurst St off-ramp. Citybound traffic from Pyrmont will be diverted via Harris St and local roads. Sections of Sussex St, Erskine St, Hickson Rd, Kent St, King St and other local streets will also be closed near King St Wharf and Barangaroo. Sections of Union St, Murray St, Pyrmont Bridge Rd, Pyrmont St, Darling Dr and surrounding local roads will be closed in Pyrmont.  
  • Dawes Point, Millers Point, The Rocks & Barangaroo – 5am to 12.30pm: Sections of Hickson Rd, Towns Pl, Dalgety Rd, Pottinger St, Windmill St, Watson Rd and surrounding streets will be closed. Access to The Rocks is maintained via York St, Lang St and Harrington St. 
  • Pyrmont – 5am to 11am: Sections of Pirrama Rd, Bowman St, Darling Island Rd, Harris St, Bank St, Refinery Dr and surrounding local roads will be closed.  
  • Pyrmont – 5am to 11.30am: Pirrama Rd between Jones Bay Rd and Murray St; and Murray St between Union St and Pirrama Rd will be closed. 
  • Sydney CBD and The Domain Sydney – from 5am: A section of College St will be closed until 9.30am; sections of Bent St, Shakespeare Pl and Sir John Young Cres will be closed from 5.30am to 12pm; and Art Gallery Rd, Hospital Rd, Mrs Macquaries Rd and Prince Albert Rd will be closed until 1pm.  

Special event clearway parking restrictions will be in effect from 2am on various roads throughout the Sydney CBD, The Domain Sydney, Circular Quay, The Rocks, Millers Point, Barangaroo and Pyrmont. All clearways will be lifted by 12.30pm and all roads will be reopened by 1pm.   

Special event clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
All closures affect both directions unless specified otherwise. 

SYDNEY CBD 

FROM 4AM TO 12.30PM 

  • Bridge St between Phillip St and Macquarie St 
  • Conservatorium Rd between Macquarie St and Government House gates 
  • Hunter St between Elizabeth St and Macquarie St 
  • Macquarie St* between Albert St and St James Rd 
  • Phillip Ln between Phillip St and Bent St 

*NOTE: Access to Sydney Opera House and Macquarie St north of Albert St is via Albert St. 

FROM 5AM TO 9.30AM 

  • College St between Prince Albert Rd and Park St (northbound) 

FROM 5AM TO 1PM 

  • Art Gallery Rd between Hospital Rd and Mrs Macquaries Rd 
  • Hospital Rd between Art Gallery Rd and Shakespeare Pl* 
  • Mrs Macquaries Rd from Art Gallery Rd (full loop) 
  • Prince Albert Rd between St Marys Rd and Hospital Rd 

FROM 5.30AM TO 12PM 

  • Bent St* between Phillip St and Macquarie St
  • Shakespeare Pl between Macquarie St and Hospital Rd
  • Sir John Young Cres from Crown St to Shakespeare Pl

*NOTE: Managed access to Wilson Parking Chifley Tower Car Park under Police control.

*NOTE: Temporary two-way traffic arrangements in place on Hospital Rd from 5am to 1pm. Entry and exit via Prince Albert Rd only, under traffic control. 

CAHILL EXPRESSWAY / SYDNEY HARBOUR BRIDGE  

FROM 4AM TO 12.30PM 

  • Cahill Expressway between Conservatorium Rd and Bradfield Hwy (Circular Quay Overpass) 
  • Cahill Expressway from Domain Tunnel on-ramp 

NOTE: Access to Sydney Harbour Bridge from Sydney CBD is via Clarence St or Kent St.  

THE ROCKS / DAWES POINT / MILLERS POINT / WALSH BAY / BARANGAROO  

FROM 5AM TO 12.30PM 

  • Argyle Place between Dalgety Road and Lower Fort St 
  • Argyle St between Kent St and Lower Fort St 
  • Bettington St* between Dalgety Road and Merriman St 
  • Dalgety Rd between Towns Pl and Argyle Pl 
  • Hickson Rd between Napoleon St and Barton St (northbound) 
  • Hickson Rd between Barton St and OPT elevated roadway  
  • High Ln* between Argyle Pl, High St and Kent St 
  • High St* between Argyle Pl and Kent St 
  • Kent St* between High St and Argyle St (northbound) 
  • Kent St between Argyle St and Windmill St 
  • Lance Ln* between High St and High Ln 
  • Merriman St* from Bettington St 
  • Munn St from High St 
  • Pottinger St^ between Hickson Rd and Windmill St 
  • Rodens Ln* from Bettington St 
  • Towns Pl between Hickson Rd and Dalgety Rd 
  • Upper Fort St from Watson Rd  
  • Watson Rd between Upper Fort St and Argyle St 
  • Windmill St between Lower Fort St and Dalgety Road 
  • Windmill St (laneway) between Argyle Place and Windmill St  

NOTES: 

*Local access available to Kent St and High St as far north as Argyle St. After 11am, access to Dalgety Rd, Bettington St, Rodens Le, Merriman St and Barangaroo car park is via High St to Argyle Ple (west of Kent St only). Kent St, south of Argyle St, southbound exit allowed for local traffic only.   

^Exit via Windmill St only allowed from Pottinger St until 6am.   

WESTERN DISTRIBUTOR  

FROM 5AM to 10.30AM  

  • Fig St on-ramp to City and Sydney Harbour Bridge 
  • King St off-ramp to City North and Sydney Harbour Bridge 
  • Pyrmont St on-ramp to City and Sydney Harbour Bridge 

Western Distributor eastbound traffic will be diverted to the Bathurst St off-ramp: access to Sydney Harbour Bridge is via Bathurst St and Kent St then the Kent St on-ramp; access to CBD North is via Clarence St. 

SYDNEY CBD / BARANGAROO / PYRMONT  

FROM 5AM to 10.30AM  

  • Barton St between Barangaroo Ave and Hickson Rd 
  • Bunn St between Pyrmont St and Murray St 
  • Darling Dr** between Murray St and Iron Wharf Pl roundabout 
  • Edward Ln between Pyrmont St and Little Edward St 
  • Edward St between Pyrmont Bridge Rd and Union St 
  • Erskine St* between Kent St and Shelley St 
  • Hickson Rd between Napoleon St and Barton St (southbound) 
  • Kent St between Margaret St and Erskine St (southbound) 
  • King St between Sussex St and Kent St 
  • Margaret St between Clarence St and Kent St (westbound) 
  • Murray St^ between Pyrmont St and Union St (northbound) 
  • Napoleon St between Hickson Rd and Kent St 
  • Pyrmont Bridge Rd between Pyrmont St and Harris St (eastbound) 
  • Pyrmont Bridge Rd between Pyrmont St and Union St 
  • Pyrmont St between Pyrmont Bridge Rd and Murray St 
  • Slip St from Sussex St

NOTES: 

*Between 6.30am and 10.30am:  

  • Local access to King St Wharf, Barangaroo South and Barangaroo Central precincts is via Harbour St to the Western Distributor northbound exit to Wheat Rd (sign-posted ‘Erskine St City North’)  
  • Restricted exit from King St Wharf and Barangaroo South and Central precincts via Wheat Rd under contra-flow for vehicles 7m or under in length (21 seat minibuses and smaller)  

^Local access maintained to Murray St south of Union St. NO access to Pyrmont Bridge Rd or Star Casino  

**Local access for residents and Ibis/ Novotel hotels only

FROM 5AM to 11AM

NOTES: 

* Resident access is maintained to Bowman St. Exit for local traffic via John St only.  

^Exit permitted via Refinery Drive to Tambua St before 6.45am and after 10.45am subject to Police discretion.  

**No access from/to Pirrama Road from Wharf 7 and Wharf 10 access roads. 

^^Access maintained for taxis to Star Casino.  

***Resident access maintained via Jones Bay Rd until 6:45am 

FROM 5AM to 11.30AM  

  • Access road to Wharf 7 & 10^ from Pirrama Rd

  • Pirrama Rd^ between Jones Bay Road and Murray St  

NOTES: 

*No access to Murray St from the Australian National Maritime Museum access road.  

^No access from/to Pirrama Road from Wharf 7 and Wharf 10 access roads. 

 

CYCLEWAY CLOSURES 

FROM 4AM TO 12.30PM 

  • Macquarie St between King St and St James Rd 
  • King St between Phillip St and Macquarie St 

FROM 5AM TO 9.30AM

FROM 5AM TO 10.30AM

  • Union St between Edward St and Murray St 
  • Sussex St between Napoleon St and Erskine St 

 FROM 5AM TO 12.30PM 

  • Hickson Rd between Munn St overpass and Barangaroo Central  

CYCLING DETOUR – 5AM TO 12PM 

Cycling detours will be in place between Kent St cycleway and the Sydney Harbour Bridge cycleway, via Kent St Sydney Harbour Bridge on-ramp to Observatory Hill. Marshals will direct cyclists across Upper Fort St in both directions when safe gaps between runners are available. Alternatively, cyclists can travel between Sydney Harbour Bridge cycleway and the Sydney CBD via the “Bridge Stairs” Tunnel and through Cumberland St.

arrangementElements.html:
Special event clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.title: Special Event Clearways

arrangementElements.html:
Motorists are advised to avoid King Street Wharf, Barangaroo and Crown Sydney precincts between 5am and 12.30pm other than for residential or essential access. Police may restrict vehicle access along closed roads, including to residents and businesses. 

Circular Quay 

  • From 4am, access to the Sydney Opera House and Macquarie Street (north of Albert Street) will be via Albert Street until Macquarie Street reopens at 12.30pm. 

Sydney Hospital & Hospital Road 

  • From 5am to 1pm, two-way access to Hospital Road is via Prince Albert Road only, under traffic control. No access from Shakespeare Place. 

King Street Wharf and Barangaroo South 

  • From 6.25am to 10.30am, access to and from King Street Wharf, Cockle Bay Wharf and Barangaroo South will be restricted to tenants/residents only. Access will be managed via Wheat Road from Harbour Street under traffic control; no access is available via Erskine Street or Sussex Street. 
  • Entry from the south via Harbour Street: continue northbound on the Western Distributor and veer left into Wheat Road at the signposted exit for Erskine Street/CBD North. 
  • Entry from Western Distributor: take the Bathurst Street exit towards Darling Harbour, turn right onto Sussex Street, right onto Liverpool Street and then Right onto Harbour Street and veer left into Wheat Road at the signposted exit for Erskine Street/CBD North. 
  • Exit the precinct via Wheat Road under contra-flow traffic control (light vehicles only). Vehicles over 7 metres (including trucks, buses and coaches) are unable to use the managed exit and will need to remain within the precinct until Erskine Street reopens. 
  • Motorists are strongly advised to avoid non-essential travel to or from these precincts between 6am and 10.30am as delays are expected. 
  • Before and after these times, access is maintained via Erskine Street at Kent Street. 

Millers Point & Barangaroo North 

  • Managed access to Kent Street and High Street properties as far north as Argyle Street is maintained throughout the event. Access via Kent Street northbound by traffic control. Kent Street southbound exit allowed for local traffic only. 
  • After 11am, access to Barangaroo Reserve & car park, Dalgety Road and Bettington Street will be available via Kent Street, High Street, Argyle Place and Dalgety Road until roads reopen at 12.30pm. 
  • Pottinger Street exit permitted via Windmill Street until 6am. 

The Rocks & Dawes Point  

  • Access maintained via Lang Street and Harrington Street throughout the event. 

Pyrmont 

  • Access to Murray Street properties/hotels and surrounding local roads permitted via Darling Drive under traffic control for light vehicles and coaches only (trucks not permitted). Note, coaches can only turn around via hotel driveways. 
  • No access available to Pyrmont Bridge Road or Star Casino from 5am to 10.30am. Access for taxis to Star Casino maintained via Jones Bay Road. 
  • Refinery Drive will be closed from 5am to 11am with exit permitted via Tambua Street before 6.35am and after 10.45am, only at Police discretion when there are safe gaps between runners. If you need access to your vehicle/property during these times, please plan ahead and relocate your vehicle prior. 
  • Darling Island Road, Wharf Crescent, Fyfe Street and Trouton Place closed from 5am to 11am with exit permitted via Jones Bay Road until 6:30am.
  • Access to and from The Star car park is via Pyrmont Street. 
  • No access from/to Pirrama Road from Wharf 7 and Wharf 10 access roads from 5am to 11.30am. 
  • No access to Murray Street from the Australian National Maritime Museum access road from 5am to 11.30am. 
  • Exit from driveways on Pyrmont Street properties between Bunn Lane and Murray Street permitted southbound under traffic control throughout the event. 
  • Resident access maintained to Bowman Street. Exit for local traffic via John Street only.
arrangementElements.title: Managed Local Access Arrangements

properties.diversions: Travel on transport is included for participants. Buses in the city and Pyrmont will be diverting and using different stops while road closures are in place. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.

roads.mainStreet: Cahill Expressway, Western Distributor, Macquarie Street, Hickson Road, The Domain Sydney

webLinks.linkText: Milton Folk, Jazz and Blues Festival

properties.otherAdvice:
The Milton Folk, Jazz and Blues Festival will take place from Friday to Sunday..

During the event there will be no access to Wason Street from the Princes Highway.
Higher than normal traffic volumes are expected in the northbound direction due to the Queensland Labour Day public holiday.

NRL Roosters v Titans at polytec Stadium in Gosford. Kick off at 6pm, full time approximately 7.50pm

Traffic will be heavier than usual on roads approaching the stadium as fans travel to and from the match. Allow extra travel time and use local parking. 

Dane Drive will be closed from 3.30pm between the Central Coast Highway and Donnison Street and will reopen once crowds have cleared the area

NRL: Dragons v Knights at WIN Stadium. Kick-off at 3pm, full time around 5pm.

Traffic will be heavier than usual on roads approaching the Wollongong CBD and on local roads as fans travel to and from the match.

Local roads around the stadium may close before, during and after the show for the safety of pedestrians.

Fans driving to the game on the freeway are encouraged to use the Masters Rd exit to avoid congestion at Mount Ousley Rd, both in and out of Wollongong.

Motorists should allow extra time for parking and be aware of increased pedestrian activity on local roads.

properties.publicTransport: Match tickets include travel on public transport. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
NRL: Bulldogs v Cowboys at Accor Stadium. NRL kick-off at 6pm. Full time around 7.45pm.

Sydney Olympic Park will be busier than usual with another event taking place at nearby Sydney Showground.

Motorists should allow extra travel time on roads including the M4 Mwy, Homebush Bay Dr, Concord Rd, Lane Cove Rd, Parramatta Rd, Centenary Dr and Silverwater Rd.

If you need to drive, pre-book parking online in advance to secure a space. Follow the directions of staff and have your parking ticket ready to show at the car park entry.

Sydney Olympic Park Authority may close some local roads to accommodate increased public transport services and ensure pedestrian safety during events. For road closure details, visit the Sydney Olympic Park interactive map for more details.

roads.mainStreet: M4 Motorway, Homebush Bay Drive, Parramatta Road, Centenary Drive

properties.publicTransport: Travel on transport is included in match tickets and additional light rail services will run. Visit transportnsw.info to plan your trip.
properties.otherAdvice:
Super Rugby: Waratahs v Western Force at Allianz Stadium on Friday 1 May. Kick-off at 7.35pm, full time aroun 9.20pm.

Traffic delays are expected on roads approaching Moore Park as rugby fans make their way to the precinct in the already busy evening peak.

Motorists should carpool and allow plenty of extra travel time on roads including the Eastern Distributor, Moore Park Rd, Cleveland St, Anzac Pde and Lang Rd.

Changed traffic conditions:

  • Driver Avenue will be partially closed outside the stadium from 4.30pm and will reopen once crowds have safely cleared the area.   
  • No through-traffic will be available on Driver Avenue, however access to EP3 grass car park and drop-off areas maintained from Lang Road. Car Parks open from 4.35pm. 
  • Special event clearways will be in place on roads in Moore Park and surrounding areas from 3pm to 11pm on sections of Albion Street and Lang Road, and from 6am to 11pm on sections of Cleveland Street and Fitzroy Street. 

Clearways are strict no-parking zones for all vehicle types, and apply even to local residents and TfNSW Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs carefully as vehicles left in clearways will be towed and a fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator.

arrangementElements.html:
Clearways are strict no-parking zones and also apply to local residents and Mobility Scheme Permit holders. Check signs before parking as vehicles left in special event clearways will be towed and a towing fee applies. If your vehicle is towed, you can search your vehicle registration at the Find My Car website to find its new location, or alternatively call 132 701 to speak to an operator. 

From 6am to 11pm

  • Cleveland Street between South Dowling Street and City Road (southern side) 
  • Cleveland Street between Abercrombie Street and South Dowling Street (northern side) 
  • Fitzroy Street between South Dowling Street and Bourke Street (northern side) 

From 3pm to 11pm

  • Albion Street between Bourke Street and Flinders Street (both sides) 
  • Lang Road between Anzac Parade and Errol Flynn Boulevard (northern side)
